columns.name: asset_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the asset associated with the vulnerability.

columns.name: cve_id
columns.description: Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures identifier for the vulnerability.

columns.name: cvss_score
columns.description: CVSS score representing the severity of the vulnerability.

columns.name: epss_score
columns.description: EPS score indicating the likelihood of exploitation.

columns.name: is_kev
columns.description: Indicates if the vulnerability is a Known Exploited Vulnerability (KEV).

columns.name: is_internet_facing
columns.description: Indicates if the asset is internet-facing.

columns.name: discovered_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the vulnerability was discovered.

columns.name: updated_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the vulnerability record was last updated.

grain: Each row represents a unique vulnerability associated with a specific asset.
data_quality_concerns: asset_id and cve_id are not nullable but should be checked for uniqueness.
cvss_score and epss_score may contain null values which could affect aggregations.
Boolean fields may have inconsistent values if not properly validated.
recommended_transformations: Ensure cvss_score and epss_score are validated to avoid nulls where applicable.
Consider creating a derived column for the age of the vulnerability based on discovered_at.
Implement checks to ensure asset_id and cve_id combinations are unique.
